{{Short description|Italian film festival in 2015}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox Film festival |name = 72nd Venice International Film Festival |image = VeniceFilmFestival72poster.jpg |caption = Festival poster |opening = ''[[Everest (2015 film)|Everest]]'' |closing = ''[[Mr. Six (film)|Mr. Six]]'' |location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] |founded = 1932 |awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[From Afar (film)|From Afar]]'' |host = [[Elisa Sednaoui]] |artistic_director=[[Alberto Barbera]] |date = 2 – 12 September 2015 |website = [http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/ Website] | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[71st Venice International Film Festival|71st]] | followed_by = [[73rd Venice International Film Festival|73rd]] }} [[File:Logo Venezia 2015.jpg|thumb|right|269x269px|72nd Venice International Film Festival]] The '''72nd annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''', was held from 2 to 12 September 2015, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]].{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/72nd-festival/line-up |title=72nd Venice International Film Festival, Line-up |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150926101732/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/72nd-festival/line-up |archive-date=26 September 2015 }} Mexican filmmaker [[Alfonso Cuarón]] was the jury president for the main competition.{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/11-05.html |title=Director Alfonso Cuarón President of the International Jury for the Venezia 72 Competition |date=11 May 2015 |access-date=11 May 2015 |work=Venice Biennale |archive-date=27 June 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150627182701/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/11-05.html |url-status=dead}} French-Italian actress [[Elisa Sednaoui]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies of the festival.{{cite web |title=Elisa Sednaoui to host the opening and closing nights of Venezia 72 |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/27-04.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150905025824/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/27-04.html |archive-date=5 September 2015 |access-date=29 July 2015 |work=Venice Biennale}} The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[From Afar (film)|From Afar]]'' by [[Lorenzo Vigas]]. The festival poster featured [[Nastassja Kinski]] in front as reminiscent of [[Wim Wenders]]'s ''[[Faraway, So Close!]]'',{{Cite web|title=Faraway, So Close! (1993)|url=https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0107209/mediaviewer/rm3103825408/|access-date=2021-06-24|website=IMDb|language=en-US}} while in the background it featured the character of [[Antoine Doinel]] portrayed by [[Jean-Pierre Léaud]] from [[François Truffaut]]'s 1959 [[drama film]] ''[[The 400 Blows]]'', which also appeared on the [[71st Venice International Film Festival]] poster.{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/venice-2015-film-festival-poster-806810|title=Venice: 2015 Film Festival Poster Unveiled|access-date=29 July 2015 |work=The Hollywood Reporter}} The festival opened with ''[[Everest (2015 film)|Everest]]'' by [[Baltasar Kormákur]],{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2015/07/baltasar-kormakurs-everest-venice-film-festival-universal-working-title-1201472041/ |title=Baltasar Kormákur’s ‘Everest’ To Open Venice Film Festival |author=Ali Jaafar |date=8 July 2015 |access-date=8 July 2015 |work=Deadline Hollywood}} and closed with ''[[Mr. Six (film)|Mr. Six]]'' by [[Guan Hu]].{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/venice-film-festival-unveils-lineup-811809|title=Venice Film Festival Unveils Star-Studded Lineup for 72nd Edition|access-date=29 July 2015}} ==Juries== === Main competition (''Venezia 72'') === *[[Alfonso Cuarón]], Mexican filmmaker - Jury President{{cite web|url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/72nd-festival/juries/ |title=International Juries of the 72nd Venice Film Festival |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150926111117/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/72nd-festival/juries/ |archive-date=26 September 2015 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.ew.com/article/2015/07/27/venice-film-festival-jury-elizabeth-banks|title=Elizabeth Banks, Diane Kruger join Alfonso Cuarón on Venice Film Festival jury|work=[[Entertainment Weekly]]|date=27 July 2015|access-date=29 July 2015}} *[[Elizabeth Banks]], American actress and director *[[Emmanuel Carrère]], French author, screenwriter and director *[[Nuri Bilge Ceylan]], Turkish director *[[Hou Hsiao-hsien]], Taiwanese filmmaker *[[Diane Kruger]], German actress *[[Francesco Munzi]], Italian director *[[Paweł Pawlikowski]], Polish filmmaker *[[Lynne Ramsay]], Scottish filmmaker === Horizons (''Orizzonti'') === *[[Jonathan Demme]], American director - Jury President{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2015/film/festivals/jonathan-demme-to-receive-visionary-talent-award-in-venice-1201580580/|title=Jonathan Demme to Receive Visionary Talent Award in Venice|work=Variety|date=28 August 2015|access-date=29 August 2015}} *[[Alix Delaporte]], French director and screenwriter *[[Fruit Chan]], Hong Kong director *[[Anita Caprioli]], Italian actress *[[Paz Vega]], Spanish actress === Opera Prima (Venice Award for a Debut Film) === *[[Saverio Costanzo]], Italian director -
